title: lupinus argenteus subsp. parviflorus
intro: Lupinus argenteus subsp. parviflorus, also known as the silvery lupine, is a subspecies of the lupine plant native to North America, characterized by its silvery-gray foliage and small, fragrant flowers. The medicinal properties of silvery lupine include its ability to act as a natural diuretic and to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits associated with this plant include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce the risk of cardiovascular disease, and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia. Herbal preparations of silvery lupine can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to support kidney function, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being.
